title: Class System — Warrior / Rogue / Sorcerer
problem: Every run is identical. Players have no identity, no pre-run strategy, no build fantasy. A three-class system (Warrior, Rogue, Sorcerer) gives each run a distinct flavor — different base stats, different skill sets, and a clear difficulty gradient that rewards mastery.
analysis: The game already has XP leveling and a mana-gated skill system but both are classless. Adding a class layer means: (1) each class gets a unique stat profile (bonusHp, defensePerLevel, bonusCritChance, bonusDex, baseMana, manaPerLevel, baseSpellPower, spellPowerPerFloor, spellPowerPerLevel, passivePerLevel), (2) each class gets 4–6 dedicated skills in unlock order, (3) ClassSelectScreen replaces the direct 'start run' flow, (4) buildPlayerStats and maxManaForLevel become class-aware. Balance target: Warrior is the easiest class (highest completion %), Rogue is mid, Sorcerer is hardest — validated by Monte Carlo simulation and fullRunSim diffTransitionSim across Normal/Nightmare/Hell difficulty tiers.
implementation: 1. src/data/classes.ts: ClassDef interface with bonusHp, defensePerLevel, bonusCritChance, bonusDex, baseMana, manaPerLevel, baseSpellPower, spellPowerPerFloor, spellPowerPerLevel, skillIds[], passivePerLevel. 2. Current class stat profiles: Warrior (bonusHp:+40, defensePerLevel:5, baseMana:50, manaPerLevel:5, passivePerLevel:{hp:25,defense:10,damage:6}); Rogue (bonusHp:-10, bonusCritChance:10, bonusDex:8, baseMana:60, manaPerLevel:6, passivePerLevel:{critChance:2,dexterity:4,damage:5}); Sorcerer (bonusHp:+5, baseMana:60, manaPerLevel:6, baseSpellPower:10, spellPowerPerLevel:7, passivePerLevel:{spellPower:10,mana:8,hp:20}). 3. src/screens/ClassSelectScreen.tsx: 3 class cards, tap to select + confirm; shows flavor text, stat preview, skill list. 4. gameStore: classId: ClassId | null, selectClass(id) action. 5. maxHpForFloor() and maxManaForLevel() both accept optional classId. 6. buildPlayerStats() uses classDef bonuses including passivePerLevel stacked per level above 10. 7. CombatScreen skill grid: fixed 2-column layout. Warrior/Rogue (4 skills) → 2×2; Sorcerer (6 skills) → 3×2. 8. Skill glyph fontSize 24 for legibility. 9. combatStore skill filter: only SKILLS where classId matches. 10. spellPower = baseSpellPower + floor×spellPowerPerFloor + level×spellPowerPerLevel (Warrior/Rogue = 0). 11. passivePerLevel: each field is multiplied by max(0, level-10) in buildPlayerStats/maxManaForLevel — gives meaningful late-game scaling without changing F1-F10 feel. 12. Monte Carlo sim (src/sim/classBalanceSim.ts) + fullRunSim + diffTransitionSim validate balance.

decisions.decision: Three classes: Warrior (tank), Rogue (burst/evasion), Sorcerer (elemental scaling)
decisions.rationale: Classic D2-inspired trinity. Covers the three playstyle archetypes (durability, speed, power) without overlap.

decisions.decision: Class selected at run start, persists for the run only — no persistent class across runs
decisions.rationale: Roguelite design: each run is a fresh identity. Cross-run class persistence would reduce variety.

decisions.decision: Each class has a fixed skillIds array defining available skills
decisions.rationale: Deterministic skill lists per class make balance tractable and skill grid layout predictable.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: sorcerer skillIds updated to include spark as first entry (L0 starter) — fills 3x2 skill grid with 6 skills
decisions.rationale: Sorcerer previously had 5 skills leaving a null placeholder. Spark fills the grid and provides a usable offensive option from floor 1.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: class descriptions revised to be more evocative — Warrior armored juggernaut, Rogue swift assassin, Sorcerer arcane caster
decisions.rationale: Original descriptions were functional but flat. More evocative copy communicates playstyle identity to a new player at the class select screen.

decisions.decision: Deep-floor passives (passivePerLevel): each class gains stacking bonuses per level above 10
decisions.rationale: fullRunSim 30-floor sim showed progression hitting a wall at F15-F20 — warrior and sorcerer had near-zero survival rates beyond F15. Passives make F11-F30 content meaningful without changing the F1-F10 experience. Each field in passivePerLevel is multiplied by max(0, level-10) and added to buildPlayerStats. Values tuned via diffTransitionSim so all 3 classes are BALANCED across Normal/Nightmare/Hell tiers.

decisions.decision: Balance pass BMAD S6: warrior bonusHp 20→40, defensePerLevel 2→5, baseMana 20→50, manaPerLevel 4→5; rogue baseMana 35→60; sorcerer bonusHp -5→+5
decisions.rationale: fullRunSim 3k-run analysis with 30-floor dungeon showed warrior and sorcerer dying before F10 with original stat values. Warrior needed more HP and defense to be the 'tank' class (target: easiest Normal clear). Rogue mana pool was starving players within 2-3 rounds. Sorcerer with -5 HP was glass-cannon to the point of being unplayable at floor 1 with no gear.

successCriteria: ClassSelectScreen shows 3 classes with clear stat differences. Selecting a class applies correct HP/mana/crit/dex bonuses. Only the selected class's skills appear in CombatScreen. Sorcerer Fireball damage scales visibly with floor depth. Monte Carlo sim shows Warrior > Rogue > Sorcerer in floor-1 naked and skilled scenarios.
knownLimitations: No class change mid-run — you commit at the ClassSelectScreen
Sorcerer's late-game advantage (deep spell power scaling) means Rogue may surpass Warrior in fully-geared min-maxed scenarios — this is a known design tradeoff
No class-specific loot affinity in v1 (e.g., Warrior doesn't see more weapon drops)
recommendations: ClassSelectScreen should show a difficulty indicator (1 skull for Warrior, 2 for Rogue, 3 for Sorcerer). TownScreen character card already shows class name with class color — keep this. Consider class-specific CodexScreen entries in a future CLASSES tab.

publicInterface.name: ClassDef
publicInterface.description: { id, name, description, flavor, color, bonusHp, defensePerLevel, bonusCritChance, bonusDex, baseMana, manaPerLevel, baseSpellPower, spellPowerPerFloor, spellPowerPerLevel, skillIds, passivePerLevel } — passivePerLevel is a partial record of {hp,defense,damage,critChance,dexterity,spellPower,mana}; each field is multiplied by max(0,level-10) and stacked into buildPlayerStats. Enables meaningful F11-F30 progression without changing F1-F10 feel.

publicInterface.name: CLASSES
publicInterface.description: Array of 3 class definitions: warrior, rogue, sorcerer

publicInterface.name: getClassDef
publicInterface.description: Returns the ClassDef for a given class ID, throws if unknown

publicInterface.name: ClassSelectScreen
publicInterface.description: Full-screen class picker, sets classId in gameStore then navigates to grid

userGuide:
## Choosing a Class

At the start of each run you pick one of three classes:

**Warrior** (easiest) — +40 HP, +5 defense/level. Tanky and forgiving. Skills: Power Strike, Battle Cry, Iron Skin, Whirlwind.

**Rogue** (medium) — -10 HP but +10% crit chance and +8 dexterity. High burst potential. Skills: Backstab, Shadow Step, Rapid Strike, Smoke Bomb.

**Sorcerer** (hardest) — +5 HP, but fragile without gear. Elemental spells scale with character level (not floor). Spell power = 10 + level×7. Skills: Spark, Fireball, Meditate, Ice Blast, Chain Lightning, Mana Shield.

Your class choice resets each run — class is not a persistent selection.

## Class Mana Pools
- Warrior: 50 + level×5 (L10 = 100 mana)
- Rogue:   60 + level×6 (L10 = 120 mana)
- Sorcerer: 60 + level×6 (L10 = 120 mana; +8/level above L10 from passive)

## Deep-Floor Passives (Level 10+)
Each level above 10 stacks one copy of the class passive:
- **Warrior**: +25 HP, +10 defense, +6 damage per level
- **Rogue**: +2% crit, +4 dex, +5 damage per level
- **Sorcerer**: +10 spell power, +8 mana, +20 HP per level

Passives make Floor 11–30 progression meaningful — a L15 warrior is noticeably tankier than L10.

## Sorcerer Level Scaling
Sorcerer's Fireball deals 3× spell power. Spell power = 10 + 7×level + 10×max(0,level-10). At L10 = 80, L15 = 165 — level up aggressively.

## Dev Testing
```
bun run src/sim/classBalanceSim.ts    # 15-scenario Monte Carlo balance check
bun run src/sim/fullRunSim.ts 2000 30 # 30-floor balance validation per class
bun run src/sim/diffTransitionSim.ts 800  # Normal→Nightmare→Hell transition balance
bun tsc --noEmit                      # TypeScript check
```

codexEntry.title: Classes
codexEntry.summary: Choose a class at the start of each run. Each class has unique stats, a different skill set, and a different difficulty curve.
mechanics: Warrior: +40 HP, +5 defense per level, 50+5L mana. Skills: Power Strike, Battle Cry, Iron Skin, Whirlwind. Deep passive: +25 HP/+10 def/+6 dmg per level above 10.
Rogue: -10 HP, +10% crit chance, +8 dexterity, 60+6L mana. Skills: Backstab, Shadow Step, Rapid Strike, Smoke Bomb. Deep passive: +2% crit/+4 dex/+5 dmg per level above 10.

Sorcerer: +5 HP, spell power = 10 + level×7, 60+6L mana (+8 mana/level above 10). Skills: Spark, Fireball, Meditate, Ice Blast, Chain Lightning, Mana Shield. Deep passive: +10 SP/+8 mana/+20 HP per level above 10.
Class is chosen at the start of each run — you can play a different class each run.
Only your class's skills appear in combat — there is no cross-class skill access.
Deep-floor passives (level > 10) stack once per additional level — a L15 character has 5 stacks.
tips: New players: start with Warrior. +40 HP base and high defense scaling make early floors forgiving.
Sorcerer requires leveling up to become powerful — farm XP aggressively. At L10 spell power = 80; at L15 = 165.
Rogue's +10% crit is active from level 0 and stacks with gear. Deep passive (+2% crit/level) compounds into dominant crit rates by L15+.
Warrior's deep passive gives +10 defense per level above 10 — the tankiest class gets even tankier in Hell difficulty.
